identity.short_name: Mixed LTE count with prime-square first digits and Legendre coupling

problem_spec.summary: Two-prime counting problem with mixed valuation constraints $v_p(x^n-1)=A$ and $v_q(x^n+1)=B$, reduced by LTE to prime-square exactness, then refined by coupling the Legendre signs of the prime-square first digits of $x^{d_p}-1$ and $x^{d_q}+1$.
ask: Let $S\subset(\mathbb{Z}/M\mathbb{Z})^\times$ be the set of classes $x\pmod M$ with $\operatorname{ord}_p(x)=d_p$, $\operatorname{ord}_q(x)=2d_q$, $v_p(x^n-1)=A$, $v_q(x^n+1)=B$, and with prime-square first digits defined by $x^{d_p}\equiv 1+pa\pmod{p^2}$ and $x^{d_q}\equiv -1+qb\pmod{q^2}$ satisfying $(\frac{a}{p})=(\frac{b}{q})$. Determine $|S|$ and output the unique $N$ with $0\le N<1000$ and $N\equiv |S|\pmod{1000}$.

parameter(s).name: p
parameter(s).purpose: prime for the difference-of-powers LTE constraint and the digit $a$ modulo $p$

parameter(s).name: q
parameter(s).purpose: prime for the sum-of-powers LTE constraint and the digit $b$ modulo $q$

parameter(s).name: d_q
parameter(s).purpose: half the prescribed even order modulo $q$, forcing $x^{d_q}\equiv -1\pmod q$

parameter(s).name: n
parameter(s).purpose: structured exponent linking order data to valuation targets

parameter(s).name: M
parameter(s).purpose: modulus enabling independent prime-power analysis and CRT recombination

invariant(s).name: order2_bridge_to_negative_one
invariant(s).statement: If $\operatorname{ord}_q(x)=2d_q$ then $x^{d_q}$ has order $2$ modulo $q$, hence $x^{d_q}\equiv -1\pmod q$ and $q\mid(x^{d_q}+1)$.

invariant(s).name: parity_gate_for_sum_LTE
invariant(s).statement: The condition $n/d_q$ is odd is required for the odd-exponent LTE formula for $v_q(x^n+1)$.

invariant(s).name: LTE_depth_reduction
invariant(s).statement: Under the order constraints and parity gate, $v_p(x^n-1)=v_p(x^{d_p}-1)+v_p(n/d_p)$ and $v_q(x^n+1)=v_q(x^{d_q}+1)+v_q(n/d_q)$.

invariant(s).name: prime_square_first_digit
invariant(s).statement: If $v_p(x^{d_p}-1)=1$ then there is a unique $a\in(\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z})^\times$ with $x^{d_p}\equiv 1+pa\pmod{p^2}$, and if $v_q(x^{d_q}+1)=1$ then there is a unique $b\in(\mathbb{Z}/q\mathbb{Z})^\times$ with $x^{d_q}\equiv -1+qb\pmod{q^2}$.

invariant(s).name: affine_first_digit_bijection
invariant(s).statement: For fixed $y_0$ modulo an odd prime $r$ with $y_0^m\equiv \pm 1\pmod r$ and $r\nmid m$, the lifts $y_0+rk$ modulo $r^2$ make the digit $(y^m\mp 1)/r$ run through all classes modulo $r$ bijectively.

invariant(s).name: quadratic_character_balance
invariant(s).statement: Exactly half of $(\mathbb{Z}/r\mathbb{Z})^\times$ are quadratic residues for an odd prime $r$.

invariant(s).name: CRT_sign_coupling
invariant(s).statement: CRT gives a bijection between residues modulo $p^{A+1}q^{B+1}$ and pairs modulo $p^{A+1}$ and $q^{B+1}$, and the only cross-condition is $(\frac{a}{p})=(\frac{b}{q})$.

essential_assumption(s): $p$ and $q$ are odd primes, so LTE applies in the standard odd-prime forms.
$n/d_q$ is odd so the sum-LTE formula applies at $q$.
$p\nmid d_p$ and $q\nmid d_q$, so the first-digit map has nonzero slope modulo the prime.
uniqueness_certificate(s): The LTE identities force the prime-square exactness conditions $v_p(x^{d_p}-1)=1$ and $v_q(x^{d_q}+1)=1$. Lemma 1 makes the first digits $a$ and $b$ uniformly distributed among nonzero residues when lifting from modulo a prime to modulo the prime square, and Lemma 2 forces an exact half split by Legendre sign. Higher lifts preserve these first digits. CRT recombines the local counts, and the sign-coupling constraint selects a unique total count, giving a unique $N$ modulo $1000$.

technique(s).name: order_count_in_cyclic_group_mod_prime
technique(s).purpose: Count residues of exact orders $d_p$ and $2d_q$ modulo $p$ and $q$.
outputs: $\varphi(d_p)$ and $\varphi(2d_q)$ counts at the prime layer

technique(s).name: order2_to_negative_one_bridge
technique(s).purpose: Force $x^{d_q}\equiv -1\pmod q$ to start the sum-of-powers valuation chain.

technique(s).name: parity_gate_for_sum_LTE
technique(s).purpose: Check that $n/d_q$ is odd so LTE for $a^m+b^m$ applies.

technique(s).name: first_digit_affine_bijection_mod_prime_square
technique(s).purpose: Show first digits modulo $r$ run bijectively, with exactly one excluded lift of higher depth.

technique(s).name: CRT_coupled_count
technique(s).purpose: Combine local counts under the sign coupling using CRT.

theorem(s).name: unique_element_of_order_2_in_cyclic_group
theorem(s).role: In $(\mathbb{Z}/q\mathbb{Z})^\times$, the unique element of order $2$ is $-1$.

theorem(s).name: LTE_difference_of_powers_for_odd_prime
theorem(s).role: If odd prime $r\mid(a-b)$ and $r\nmid ab$ then $v_r(a^m-b^m)=v_r(a-b)+v_r(m)$.

theorem(s).name: LTE_sum_of_powers_for_odd_prime_odd_exponent
theorem(s).role: If odd prime $r\mid(a+b)$, $r\nmid ab$, and $m$ is odd then $v_r(a^m+b^m)=v_r(a+b)+v_r(m)$.

theorem(s).name: binomial_expansion_mod_prime_square
theorem(s).role: $(y_0+rk)^m\equiv y_0^m+m y_0^{m-1}rk\pmod{r^2}$; gives the affine digit map.

theorem(s).name: count_quadratic_residues_mod_prime
theorem(s).role: Exactly $(r-1)/2$ nonzero residues are quadratic residues modulo an odd prime $r$.

theorem(s).name: chinese_remainder_theorem_coprime_moduli
theorem(s).role: CRT gives a bijection for coprime moduli and enables product counting with a coupling filter.

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Use local-to-global structure: reduce global congruence and valuation conditions to independent prime-power constraints, then recombine via CRT with a character-level coupling invariant.

critical_trick(s): Use mixed LTE: difference at $p$ and sum at $q$ unlocked by an even-order bridge and an odd-exponent parity gate.
Introduce prime-square first digits and force a Legendre-sign coupling, turning the count into a signed CRT product.

library(s).name: itertools
library(s).reason: Enumerate units modulo $p^2$ and $q^2$ to verify local signed counts.

task(s).name: local_prime_square_enumeration
task(s).reason: Enumerate all units modulo $13^2$ and $31^2$, filter by order conditions modulo $13$ and $31$, check prime-square exactness for $x^3-1$ and $x^{15}+1$, compute the digits $a$ and $b$, compute Legendre symbols by Euler's criterion, and tally signed counts. Multiply by lift factors $13^2$ and $31^2$ and apply the CRT sign coupling to recover $|S|$.
